channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Domo.AI vision: Agentic workflows that create value
item.description: AI can do much more than make individual employees more productive—with a thoughtful strategy, it can transform your industry. To turn this vision into reality, businesses need AI solutions that are not only ambitious but also practical and adaptable. That’s where Domo steps in. Domo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft joins the European cloud group that sued it for antitrust
item.description: Microsoft seems to be a believer in the adage that if you can’t beat them, join them. Shortly after Microsoft executives settled antitrust claims with CISPE, a major European cloud organization, last summer, they began negotiating with the group to become a member.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This app locks you out of social media until you go for a walk
item.description: If cutting down on doomscrolling and increasing your step count are on your list of New Year’s resolutions for 2025, then I have the app for you.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cloud repatriation – how to balance repatriation effectively and securely
item.description: Public cloud is set to increase in popularity, however some are moving to private cloud. Here are the pros and cons of both.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sorry America! A VPN might not be a quick solution to your TikTok withdrawal after all
item.description: Experts recorded VPN spikes of up to 1566% within minutes of the block being enforced. Here's why it wasn't enough to bypass the US TikTok ban.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Resident Evil 6 Could Be Getting a Re-Release on Modern Consoles
item.description: An ESRB rating for the game has been assigned for 'Xbox Series' platforms, suggesting a possible re-release of the 2012 action horror game on modern consoles.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Las empresas son más conscientes de la huella medioambiental de la IA generativa, pero tienen problemas para abordarla
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa tiene un creciente y significativo impacto negativo en el medioambiente. A pesar de ello, muchas organizaciones no están haciendo un seguimiento adecuado de la huella que generan, lo que está poniendo en peligro sus objetivos ESG.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI and cybersecurity: A double-edged sword
item.description: In the ever-changing landscape of digital threats, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as both a formidable ally and a dangerous adversary.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AMD reveals RX 9070 GPUs won’t go on sale until March, which might be too late to prevent Nvidia cleaning up with RTX 5070 models
item.description: A losing move in the next-gen GPU war? Perhaps, though Nvidia may have its own issues, if rumors are right.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung Set to Revolutionize Foldables with Four New Models in 2025 - Tech Update
item.description: Samsung is reportedly set to launch four new foldable smartphones in 2025, including a tri-fold model that will hit the market later this year. The tech giant's plans for its foldable lineup were revealed through leaks and rumors.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Trump charges DOGE with modernizing federal technology and software
item.description: DOGE, the whimsically named Department of Government Efficiency proposed by Elon Musk as a way to save $2 trillion in US federal government spending, is now a reality.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Don’t buy a new iPad Air – a new M3 model has been tipped to land early this year
item.description: A new handful of rumors seem to corroborate the upcoming release of an M3-powered iPad Air, the first tablet to feature the chipset after the iPad Pro skipped straight to the M4.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: AI can predict career success from a facial image, study finds
item.description: A new study by researchers from four universities claims artificial intelligence (AI) models can predict career and educational success from a single image of a person’s face.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump shifted from banning TikTok to saving it
item.description: During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed a threat to U.S. national security. But on the eve of his return to the White House, the president-elect is being hailed as the app’s savior.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy set to depart DOGE
item.description: Biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy confirmed on Monday he will not serve on President Trump’s “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE) commission, leaving Tesla CEO Elon Musk to singularly lead the advisory group.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How to manage photos, videos on new laptop with limited space
item.description: With limited hard drive space, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers these tips for storing large video files and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: The price of bitcoin has surged to over $109,000 just hours ahead of President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration, as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by him soon after he takes office.
item.dc:creator: Alan Suderman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok ban upheld by Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok and ByteDance — along with some content creators and users — argued the law violates constitutionally protected free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple satellite patent takes big step toward stable communication
item.description: Apple has big plans for satellite services, a new patent filing confirms. It’s the latest nugget of information to roll down Apple Confidential mountain, a plan to make it much easier for devices to maintain connectivity as they move between satellites.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fox will stream next month's Super Bowl for free on Tubi
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fox will stream the Super Bowl on Tubi for the first time, giving streamers and cord cutters a chance to watch the big game on Feb. 9 for free. Tubi was acquired by Fox Corporation in 2020. It is a free, ad-supported streaming service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft creates new internal unit for AI development
item.description: Microsoft is creating a new engineering group focused on artificial intelligence: Core AI — Platform and Tools. The group combines the company’s Developer Division with the AI Platforms work teams and some employees who previously reported to Microsoft’s CTO.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Get ready to experience thrill of eVTOL racing
item.description: The Jetson One eVTOL showcases air racing potential, ushering in a new aviation era. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the race for eVTOL supremacy is just beginning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEC sues Elon Musk, saying he didn't disclose Twitter ownership on time before buying it
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has sued billionaire Elon Musk, saying he failed to disclose his ownership of Twitter stock in a timely manner in early 2022, before buying the social media site.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation
